title: Filename Sidecar (entity-discovery)
description: Sidecar JSON co-written by TWO emitters across the ingestion pipeline. (1) pre_03_inbox_organization writes the NORMALIZER fields (schema_version..bucket + tokens_dropped) BEFORE any destructive filename normalization. (2) /ingest slash orchestrator (scripts/ingest-with-entity-discovery.py) writes the ENTITY DECISION fields (decision, destination, routing_reason, gemini_used) after Speaker Visual Gate + Entity Discovery. Last-writer-wins is SAFE because the two emitters touch disjoint field groups. See STORY-MCE-ROUND-TRIP AC-1 (G4) + AC-5 (G5), workflow-dag-2026-05-13 N1 + N5, composition-engineer sign-off 2026-05-13 (condition C-N5-2 Option A).

schema_version.description: Semver of this sidecar shape. Bump on any breaking change. v1.0.0 = normalizer-only fields (N1). v1.1.0 = merged normalizer + entity decision fields (N1 + N5).

emitted_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this sidecar was written.

emitter.description: Logical identifier of the writer (module + symbol). Example: 'engine.intelligence.pipeline.inbox_organizer.organize_inbox'.

filename_original.description: Verbatim source filename (basename only, including extension). Must be byte-equal to the name as it landed in the inbox before any normalization.

filename_normalized.description: Filename the organizer/router intends to use after normalization. May equal filename_original when the normalizer is a no-op.

tokens_dropped.description: Tokens present in filename_original that are NOT present in filename_normalized. Empty list means the normalizer was non-destructive. NEVER null when a normalization step ran — downstream routers must be able to distinguish 'no tokens dropped' from 'we never checked'.

tokens_kept.description: Cleaned tokens that survived normalization. Optional companion to tokens_dropped for diagnostics.

entity_candidates.description: Entity slugs the writer believes the file is about (best effort). Routers may use the FIRST element as the explicit entity hint, overriding the legacy first-token heuristic. Empty list means 'no opinion'.

entity_evidence.description: Optional structured evidence supporting entity_candidates. Free-form: persons/companies/groups from parse_filename_evidence(), confidence, etc.

original_path.description: Absolute path of the file at the moment the sidecar was emitted. Diagnostic field for forensic tracing.

bucket.description: Knowledge bucket the file is currently in (or will be moved to).

normalizer_rule.description: Identifier of the normalization rule applied (or 'noop' when no normalization was performed). Allows downstream consumers to audit which rule mutated the filename.

notes.description: Optional free-form note from the emitter (e.g., 'fail-open: skipped sidecar write because parent dir was read-only').

ingested_at.description: [N5 portion] ISO timestamp when /ingest slash orchestrator routed the file. Distinct from emitted_at (N1) which tracks pre_03 sidecar write.

decision.description: [N5 portion] Output of infer_entities() — full entity-discovery decision after Gemini Speaker Visual Gate (when available) + filename evidence reconciliation. Persisted for audit/replay.

destination.description: [N5 portion] Absolute path the orchestrator placed the file at. Diagnostic / forensic field.

routing_reason.description: [N5 portion] Human-readable reason the orchestrator picked destination.

gemini_used.description: [N5 portion] True iff Speaker Visual Gate ran against Gemini and returned a non-null result. False on --skip-gemini, no GEMINI_API_KEY, non-video input, or exception.

gemini_bypassed_reason.description: [N5 portion] When gemini_used=false, explicit reason. Examples: 'GEMINI_API_KEY absent', '--skip-gemini flag', 'non-video file', 'exception during call'.
